data.title: VeChain (VET) remains at $0.02 post Franklin Templeton inclusion. RSI at 43.43 while MACD indicates bearish trend.
data.description: Following the integration of the Franklin Templeton fund, the price of VET has stabilized at $0.02. Technical analysis reveals a neutral RSI of 43.43, indicating no clear overbought or oversold conditions. The bearish MACD momentum hints at potential consolidation in the near future.

data.title: ATI Physical Therapy transitions from public to private ownership for long-term growth and operational flexibility.
data.description: ATI Physical Therapy, located in Downers Grove, Illinois, has recently transitioned from being publicly traded to privately owned as of August 1, 2025. The move from public to private ownership is aimed at supporting the long-term growth of the company, enhancing operational flexibility, and maintaining a strong focus on providing excellent patient care. Previously trading under the symbol ATIP on the OTC Markets, ATI Physical Therapy's shift in ownership reflects a strategic decision to prioritize sustained development and dedicated patient services.

data.title: Western Funding launches first-ever asset-backed securitization with $400 million issuance, marked by 4 note classes.
data.description: Western Funding, Inc. (WFIM), a subsidiary of Westlake Technology Holdings, achieved a significant milestone by successfully completing its first asset-backed securitization (ABS), WEFUN 2025-1, worth $400 million. The ABS consisted of four note classes: A worth $206,627,000 with an average life of 2.35 years and an interest rate of 4.75%, B worth $75,000,000 with an average life of 2.89 years and an interest rate of 4.98%, C worth $82,500,000 with an average life of 3.26 years and an interest rate of 5.34%, and D worth $35,873,000 with an average life of 3.46 years and an interest rate of 5.79%. This accomplishment represents a significant step in Western Funding's corporate development.

data.title: Ocean Bank's assets reach $7.25 billion, loans at $5.69 billion, and deposits at $6.13 billion, showing continued growth. President expresses gratitude to the team.
data.description: In Miami, it was reported by Ocean Bank that as of the second quarter ending on June 30, total assets have surged to $7.25 billion, showcasing an 8 percent rise from the prior year's end and a 13 percent increase from the second quarter of 2024. The total loans also saw an uptrend, reaching $5.69 billion, and deposits climbed to $6.13 billion as of June 30. Ocean Bank's President Chairman and CEO, Agostinho Alfonso Macedo, expressed gratitude and pride towards the Ocean Bank team for their efforts that led the bank to surpass the $7 billion asset milestone.

data.description: The stock of JASMY is currently trading at $0.02 following a 3.4% decrease in its daily value. Despite a recent advancement in integrating blockchain technology with cancer treatment, technical indicators suggest neutral momentum for the stock.

data.title: Mazda NA reported July sales of 45,057 vehicles, up 13% from last year. YTD sales: 255,355, up 5.4%.
data.description: In Irvine, California on August 1, 2025, Mazda North American Operations (MNAO) announced that their total sales for July reached 45,057 vehicles, marking a 13% increase from July 2024. In the year-to-date count, sales amounted to 255,355 vehicles, reflecting a 5.4% rise compared to the previous year.

data.title: SD-WAN market expected to reach $21.67B by 2030, growing from $7.91B in 2025 at a CAGR of 22.3%.
data.description: In Delray Beach, Florida, a report by MarketsandMarkets™ predicts significant growth for the Software-defined Wide Area Network Market, with an expected increase to USD 21.67 billion by 2030 from its current value of USD 7.91 billion in 2025. This growth represents a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 22.3% projected from 2025 to 2030. data.title: Publix's Q2 2025 sales: $15.6B, up 7.3% from 2024. Comparable store sales up 6%. Easter effect +1% on sales increase.
data.description: In Lakeland, Florida, Publix reported sales of $15.6 billion for the quarter ending June 28, 2025, marking a 7.3% rise from $14.5 billion in 2024. Comparable store sales for the same period increased by 6%. The company attributes the sales growth of the quarter primarily to the shift of the Easter holiday from the first quarter in 2024 to the second quarter in 2025, estimating a 1% increase in sales due to this timing difference. Additionally, there was an increase in net earnings, but the specific figure was not provided in the given information.

data.title: Exxon Mobil reports record production and $7.1 billion earnings in Q2 2025, surpassing estimates. Upstream production hits 4.6M boe/d.
data.description: Exxon Mobil Corporation (NYSE: XOM) disclosed a strong financial performance for the second quarter of 2025, with earnings standing at $7.1 billion, equivalent to $1.64 per share. This surpasses the $1.47 estimate put forth by analysts. Revenues for the company totaled $81.51 billion during the same period, exceeding the projected $79.34 billion. Notably, operational success was highlighted by achieving the highest second-quarter Upstream production levels post the Exxon-Mobil merger more than 25 years ago. The total production stood at 4.6 million barrels of oil equivalent per day, marking a 13% rise from the first half of 2024. This growth was mainly propelled by the Pioneer Natural Resources acquisition and record-breaking output from the Permian Basin. In the Upstream segment, earnings for the second quarter reached $5.4 billion, although it was slightly lower than the previous quarter due to weaker commodity prices. Nevertheless, volume expansion from strategic assets like the Permian Basin helped counteract these challenges. Energy Products reported second-quarter earnings of $1.4 billion.
